findings: Found 1 new medium-severity testing gap: the events.jsonl G11 invariant test still preserves an rc.37 metric-event overlap in v2.2 instead of enforcing the documented zero-overlap post-cutover contract.
issues_found: [{"title":"Events JSONL G11 invariant test still asserts rc37 metric-counter overlap","severity":"medium","description":"events-jsonl-gates.ts documents G11 as the structural invariant that every METRIC_COUNTER_NAMES value must not appear in eventLedgerEventSchema, and the package is now version 2.2.0-rc.5. The sibling test still says the post-rc.38 cutover should flip to zero overlap but asserts expect(overlap.length).toBe(counterNames.size), so the suite stays green while the forbidden dual-write overlap remains and can mask regressions in metric/event separation.","location":"packages/server/src/services/events-jsonl-gates.test.ts:152","fix_direction":"Flip the G11 test to expect(overlap).toEqual([]) for the current v2.2 line, remove metric counter event types from the event ledger schema, and adjust the G8 leak test/implementation to detect raw metric-event rows without depending on those forbidden rows parsing as valid typed ledger events.","affected_components":["packages/server/src/services/events-jsonl-gates.test.ts","packages/server/src/services/events-jsonl-gates.ts","packages/shared/src/schemas/event-ledger.ts","packages/server/src/services/metrics.ts"],"perspective":"negative-boundary-and-error-matrix"}]
